channel.title: redteams.ai — The AI Red Teaming Wiki
channel.description: Expert-level AI red teaming knowledge base

item.title: Finding CVEs with AI Red Teaming: A Research-Backed Guide
item.description: How AI red teaming techniques are discovering real-world CVEs in SQLite, OpenSSL, Linux kernel, and UEFI bootloaders — with references to the research behind it.

item.title: 250 Poisoned Documents Is All It Takes: Anthropic's Data Poisoning Breakthrough
item.description: Anthropic, the UK AI Safety Institute, and the Turing Institute proved that injecting just 250 malicious documents into pretraining data can backdoor LLMs from 600M to 13B parameters. Here's what this means for model security.

item.title: Hacking the AI Hackers: When Security Tools Become the Vulnerability
item.description: New research achieves 100% prompt injection success against AI-powered security tools. If your SOC uses AI for threat detection, your AI can be turned against you. Here's what the research found and how to defend.

item.title: LLM Jailbreaking in 2026: 97% Success Rates, Autonomous Attacks, and the Arms Race That Isn't Working
item.description: Nature Communications confirms AI reasoning models can autonomously jailbreak other LLMs with 97% success. JBFuzz achieves 99% in 60 seconds. Here's what the latest 2026 research reveals about the state of AI safety — and why current defenses are failing.

item.title: Red Teaming the AI SOC: Why Your Autonomous Security Operations Need an Adversary
item.description: As organizations rush to deploy agentic AI in their SOCs, red teamers are finding that the defenders' own AI agents are now the attack surface. 520 tool misuse incidents, memory poisoning persistence, and a 97% jailbreak success rate — here's how to red team the AI-powered SOC before attackers do.

item.title: Agent Delegation Attacks
item.description: Exploiting multi-agent delegation patterns to achieve lateral movement, privilege escalation, and command-and-control through impersonation and insecure inter-agent communication.

item.title: Agent Goal Hijacking
item.description: Techniques for redirecting AI agent objectives through poisoned inputs, indirect prompt injection, and multi-step manipulation -- the #1 ranked risk in OWASP's 2026 Agentic Top 10.

item.title: Agent Identity and Credential Theft
item.description: Exploiting how AI agents authenticate to external services -- credential theft through agent manipulation, MFA bypass, and impersonation attacks including BodySnatcher and CVE-2025-64106.

item.title: Agent Memory Poisoning
item.description: Techniques for injecting malicious content into agent memory systems -- conversation history, RAG stores, and vector databases -- to achieve persistent cross-session compromise.

item.title: Agent Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: Compromising AI agents through poisoned packages, backdoored MCP servers, malicious model registries, and weaponized agent frameworks -- including the Postmark MCP breach and NullBulge campaigns.

item.title: Autonomous Goal Drift
item.description: How autonomous AI agents drift from intended objectives through reward hacking, specification gaming, safety system bypass, and cascading failures in multi-agent systems.

item.title: Function Calling Exploitation
item.description: Practical attacks against OpenAI function calling, Anthropic tool use, and similar APIs -- injecting function calls through prompt injection, exploiting parameter validation gaps, and chaining calls.

item.title: MCP Authentication Gaps: Securing MCP Server Authentication
item.description: A defense-focused guide to understanding authentication weaknesses in MCP server deployments -- 38% of scanned servers lack any authentication -- and implementing robust token-based auth, mTLS, and middleware-based access control.

item.title: MCP Command Injection: Understanding and Preventing Shell Injection in MCP Servers
item.description: A defense-focused guide to understanding how command injection vulnerabilities arise in MCP server implementations, analyzing CVE-2025-6514 (CVSS 9.6), and implementing robust input sanitization, parameterized commands, and sandboxing to protect MCP deployments.

item.title: MCP Cross-Client Data Leak: Preventing Session Isolation Failures
item.description: A defense-focused guide to understanding and preventing data leaks across MCP client sessions, analyzing CVE-2026-25536 and implementing session-isolated state management to protect multi-tenant MCP deployments.

item.title: MCP Denial of Wallet: Preventing Token Consumption Attacks
item.description: A defense-focused guide to understanding denial-of-wallet attacks via MCP -- how malicious servers create overthinking loops causing 142.4x token amplification -- and implementing budget controls, rate limiting, and cost monitoring to protect LLM endpoints.

item.title: MCP Path Traversal: Preventing File System Escapes in MCP Servers
item.description: A defense-focused guide to preventing path traversal vulnerabilities in MCP file operations -- 82% of implementations use file operations prone to traversal -- with working filesystem sandboxing, path validation, chroot jails, and detection rules.

item.title: MCP Security: Understanding and Defending the Protocol
item.description: A comprehensive guide to Model Context Protocol security — understanding the attack surface, known vulnerabilities (30+ CVEs in early 2026), and implementing robust defenses for MCP-enabled AI agent systems.

item.title: MCP Security Testing: How to Test MCP Servers for Vulnerabilities
item.description: A defense-focused guide to security testing MCP server implementations -- methodology for MCP security assessments, scanning tools, common test cases for auth bypass, injection, traversal, and data leaks, with working test scripts and reporting templates.

item.title: MCP Server Hardening Guide: Complete Deployment Security
item.description: A comprehensive hardening guide for MCP server deployments -- covering a 24-item security checklist, Docker isolation, Nginx reverse proxy configuration, logging and monitoring setup, and network policy enforcement with working configurations for every component.

item.title: MCP Supply Chain Security: Defending Against Backdoored MCP Packages
item.description: A defense-focused guide to securing the MCP package supply chain -- analyzing the Postmark MCP breach, understanding how malicious MCP servers are distributed, and implementing package verification, dependency scanning, and policy enforcement.

item.title: Rogue and Shadow Agents
item.description: How compromised, misaligned, or unauthorized AI agents operate within systems -- rogue agents that act harmfully while appearing legitimate, and shadow agents deployed without security review.

item.title: Tool Use Exploitation
item.description: Comprehensive techniques for exploiting how AI agents call external tools and APIs, including tool description poisoning, overly permissive access abuse, and tool output manipulation.

item.title: Zero-Click Agent Compromise
item.description: Attacks where AI agents are compromised without any user interaction -- processing malicious content triggers autonomous execution, data exfiltration, and system compromise.

item.title: AI Supply Chain Incident Response
item.description: Defense-focused guide to responding to AI supply chain compromises, covering incident response playbooks, model tampering detection, rollback procedures, communication templates, and automated integrity monitoring.

item.title: AI Supply Chain Security Overview
item.description: Comprehensive overview of the AI/ML supply chain attack surface, covering model poisoning, data poisoning, dependency attacks, and risk assessment frameworks aligned with OWASP LLM03:2025.

item.title: Dependency Scanning for AI/ML
item.description: Defense-focused guide to scanning AI/ML dependencies for vulnerabilities, covering AI-specific dependency risks, malicious package detection, automated scanning pipelines, and policy enforcement for ML toolchains.

item.title: ML Pipeline Security
item.description: Defense-focused guide to securing ML training and deployment pipelines, covering CI/CD cross-tenant attacks, safetensors conversion hijacking, pipeline hardening, and isolated build environments.

item.title: Model Repository Security
item.description: Defense-focused guide to securing model downloads from public repositories like Hugging Face, covering backdoored model detection, namespace attacks, signature verification, and safe download procedures.

item.title: Model Signing and Verification
item.description: Defense-focused guide to implementing cryptographic model signing and verification, covering Sigstore for ML, certificate management, SBOM generation for AI systems, and deployment-time verification workflows.

item.title: Training Data Integrity
item.description: Defense-focused guide to ensuring training data has not been poisoned, covering label flipping, backdoor insertion, clean-label attacks, data validation pipelines, provenance tracking, and anomaly detection.

item.title: Trojan Model Detection
item.description: Defense-focused guide to detecting backdoored and trojan AI models, covering BadEdit, TrojanPuzzle, PoisonGPT techniques and practical detection methods including activation analysis, weight inspection, and behavioral testing.

item.title: OpenClaw: Anatomy of 2026's First Major AI Agent Security Crisis
item.description: How OpenClaw's meteoric rise to the most-starred GitHub project exposed critical agentic AI vulnerabilities — from ClawJacked WebSocket hijacking (CVE-2026-25253) to malicious skills distributing macOS stealers. What red teamers and defenders need to know.

item.title: AI Red Team Evidence Collection
item.description: Systematic evidence collection methodologies for AI red team engagements, including artifact preservation, finding documentation, and chain of custody procedures.

item.title: API Key Compromise Investigation
item.description: Investigating AI API key compromise incidents including detection, scope assessment, usage forensics, and remediation procedures.

item.title: Cloud AI Forensics: AWS
item.description: Forensic investigation techniques for AWS AI services including SageMaker, Bedrock, and associated infrastructure logging and evidence collection.

item.title: Cloud AI Forensics: Azure
item.description: Forensic investigation techniques for Azure AI services including Azure OpenAI, Azure ML, and Cognitive Services with diagnostic logging and evidence collection.

item.title: LLM Conversation Forensics
item.description: Forensic analysis techniques for investigating LLM conversation logs, detecting manipulation patterns, and reconstructing attack timelines from chat histories.

item.title: Model Drift Forensics
item.description: Forensic techniques for distinguishing natural model drift from deliberate tampering, including statistical detection methods and evidence collection.

item.title: Prompt Injection Chain Analysis
item.description: Analyzing chains of prompt injection attacks across multi-step AI systems, including indirect injection propagation, agentic exploitation, and cross-system attack correlation.

item.title: Vector Database Forensics
item.description: Forensic analysis techniques for detecting and investigating vector database poisoning, unauthorized modifications, and data integrity violations.

item.title: Capstone: Design and Implement an AI Safety Benchmark Suite
item.description: Build a comprehensive, reproducible benchmark suite for evaluating LLM safety across multiple risk dimensions including toxicity, bias, hallucination, and adversarial robustness.

item.title: Capstone: Design an Enterprise AI Security Program
item.description: Architect a comprehensive enterprise AI security program spanning governance, technical controls, risk management, and incident response for organizations deploying LLMs at scale.

item.title: Capstone: Build an LLM Vulnerability Tracking Database
item.description: Design and implement a structured vulnerability tracking database for cataloging, scoring, and querying LLM-specific security weaknesses across models and deployments.

item.title: Capstone: Security Audit of an Open-Source LLM
item.description: Conduct a comprehensive security audit of an open-source large language model, covering model weights integrity, safety alignment evaluation, supply chain verification, and adversarial robustness testing.

item.title: Case Study: ChatGPT Plugin Security Vulnerabilities
item.description: Analysis of security vulnerabilities discovered in the ChatGPT plugin ecosystem, including OAuth hijacking, cross-plugin data exfiltration, and prompt injection through plugin responses.

item.title: Case Study: GitHub Copilot Generating Vulnerable Code
item.description: Analysis of research findings demonstrating that GitHub Copilot and similar AI code assistants systematically generate code containing security vulnerabilities, and the implications for software supply chain security.

item.title: Case Study: Prompt Injection Attacks on Google Bard/Gemini
item.description: Analysis of prompt injection vulnerabilities discovered in Google Bard (later Gemini), including indirect injection through Google Workspace integration and the unique attack surface created by multimodal capabilities.

item.title: Network Isolation for Cloud AI Workloads
item.description: Implementing network isolation strategies for cloud AI deployments including private endpoints, VPC configurations, service mesh integration, and data plane segmentation for LLM inference and training workloads.

item.description: Understanding the division of security responsibilities between cloud providers and customers for AI/ML workloads across AWS, Azure, and GCP, with specific guidance for LLM deployments.

item.title: The Attacker Moves Second Problem
item.description: Why static LLM defenses fail against adaptive adversaries: analysis of 12 bypassed defenses and implications for defense design.

item.title: Fine-Tuning-as-a-Service Attack Surface
item.description: How API-based fine-tuning services can be exploited with minimal data and cost to remove safety alignment, including the $0.20 GPT-3.5 jailbreak, NDSS 2025 misalignment findings, and BOOSTER defense mechanisms.

item.title: Quantization & Safety Alignment
item.description: How model quantization disproportionately degrades safety alignment: malicious quantization attacks, token-flipping, and safety-aware quantization defenses.

item.title: Reasoning Model Jailbreaks
item.description: How reasoning capabilities create novel jailbreak surfaces: chain-of-thought exploitation, scratchpad attacks, and why higher reasoning effort increases attack success.

item.title: Sleeper Agents: Training-Time Backdoors
item.description: Comprehensive analysis of Hubinger et al.'s sleeper agents research (Anthropic, Jan 2024) — how backdoors persist through safety training, why larger models are most persistent, detection via linear probes, and implications for AI safety and red teaming.

item.title: Regulatory & Standards Landscape 2026
item.description: Comprehensive mapping of the 2026 AI regulatory landscape including EU AI Act Article 55, NIST AI RMF, MITRE ATLAS, and OWASP Top 10 for LLMs, with compliance checklists, penalty structures, and regulatory timelines.

item.title: Compliance Violations
item.description: Regulatory violations from AI systems including GDPR PII leakage, HIPAA violations via medical chatbots, EU AI Act penalties, and cross-border data flow issues.

item.title: Data Corruption
item.description: How compromised LLM outputs corrupt downstream systems through database pollution, cascading pipeline failures, RAG feedback loops, and automated decision poisoning.

item.title: Denial of Service
item.description: LLM resource exhaustion attacks including sponge examples, context window flooding, recursive prompt loops, and token amplification that degrade or disable AI systems.

item.title: Financial Fraud
item.description: AI-assisted financial scams including LLM-powered phishing at scale, deepfake CEO fraud, automated social engineering, credential harvesting, and financial document forgery.

item.title: Harmful Content Generation
item.description: Bypassing safety mechanisms to generate dangerous content including weapons instructions, malware code, and harassment templates, with analysis of attack patterns and defenses.

item.title: Impact Categories
item.description: Overview of the real-world consequences of successful AI attacks, from misinformation and harmful content to financial fraud and regulatory violations.

item.title: Misinformation Generation
item.description: Weaponizing LLMs to produce convincing false content at scale, including fake articles, automated propaganda, and hallucination exploitation.

item.title: Reputation Damage
item.description: Attacks that damage an organization's reputation through AI systems, including brand impersonation, offensive chatbot outputs, and manipulated public-facing bots.

item.title: AI Supply Chain Deep Dive
item.description: Deep analysis of AI supply chain security threats including sleeper agents, slopsquatting, malicious model uploads, pickle deserialization exploits, and model provenance verification challenges.

item.title: Activation Manipulation & Safety Bypass
item.description: How identifying and suppressing safety-critical activations, refusal direction vectors, and activation steering techniques can bypass safety alignment with near-100% success rates, including the IRIS technique from NAACL 2025.

item.title: KV Cache & Prompt Caching Attacks
item.description: How KV cache poisoning, prefix caching exploitation, cache timing side channels, and multi-tenant isolation failures create attack vectors in LLM serving infrastructure.

item.title: Logit Bias Exploitation
item.description: How API logit bias parameters can be abused to force specific token generation, bypass safety alignment, suppress refusal tokens, and extract model behavior through systematic probing.

item.title: Sampling Parameter Attacks
item.description: How manipulation of temperature, top-p, top-k, frequency penalties, and seed parameters can degrade safety alignment, enable reproducibility attacks, and bypass content filtering.

item.title: Audio Modality Attacks
item.description: Comprehensive attack taxonomy for audio-enabled LLMs: adversarial audio generation, voice-based prompt injection, cross-modal split attacks, and ultrasonic perturbations.

item.title: Conversation Steering
item.description: Techniques for gradually redirecting conversation context toward attack objectives without triggering safety mechanisms.

item.title: Few-Shot Manipulation
item.description: Using crafted in-context examples to steer model behavior, including many-shot jailbreaking, poisoned demonstrations, and example-based conditioning.

item.title: Language Switching
item.description: Exploiting language-specific gaps in safety training by switching to low-resource languages, mixing languages, or using transliteration to evade filters.

item.title: Many-Shot Jailbreaking
item.description: Power-law scaling of in-context jailbreaks: why 5 shots fail but 256 succeed, context window size as attack surface, and mitigations for long-context exploitation.

item.title: Multi-Turn Attacks
item.description: Attacks that span multiple conversation turns using gradual escalation, context building, crescendo patterns, and trust establishment over time.

item.title: Persona Establishment
item.description: Creating persistent alternate identities that survive across conversation turns, including character locking, identity anchoring, and progressive persona building.

item.title: Role-Play Attacks
item.description: Establishing alternate personas or fictional scenarios that cause models to bypass safety training, including DAN variants, character hijacking, and narrative framing.

item.title: Social Engineering of AI
item.description: Manipulating AI systems through emotional appeals, authority claims, urgency framing, and social pressure tactics that exploit instruction-following tendencies.

item.title: Chunk Boundary Attacks
item.description: Exploiting document splitting and chunking mechanisms in RAG pipelines, including payload injection at chunk boundaries, cross-chunk instruction injection, and chunk size manipulation.

item.title: Embedding Inversion
item.description: Recovering original text from embedding vectors, privacy implications of stored embeddings, model inversion attacks on vector databases, and embedding space analysis techniques.

item.title: Knowledge Base Poisoning (Rag Data Attacks)
item.description: Advanced corpus poisoning strategies for RAG systems, including black-box and white-box approaches, scaling dynamics, and the PoisonedRAG finding that 5 texts in millions achieve 90% attack success.

item.title: Metadata Injection
item.description: Manipulating document metadata to influence RAG retrieval ranking, bypass filtering, spoof source attribution, and exploit metadata-based access controls.

item.title: Retrieval Manipulation (Rag Data Attacks)
item.description: Techniques for manipulating RAG retrieval to control which documents reach the LLM context, including adversarial query reformulation, retriever bias exploitation, and semantic similarity gaming.

item.title: Automated Red Teaming Tools Comparison
item.description: Comprehensive comparison of automated AI red teaming tools including PyRIT, Garak, DeepTeam, AutoRedTeamer, HarmBench, and ART, with detailed capability matrices, strengths analysis, and use case recommendations.

item.title: API Orchestrator Attacks
item.description: Attack techniques targeting AI agents that orchestrate multiple API calls, including parameter injection across API chains, confused deputy attacks in multi-API workflows, and exploiting trust relationships between orchestrated services.

item.title: Browser Agent Exploitation
item.description: Attack techniques targeting AI agents that control web browsers, including DOM injection, navigation hijacking, credential theft, and cross-origin exploitation of browser-controlling agents.

item.title: Code Agent Manipulation
item.description: Techniques for manipulating AI agents that generate, execute, and review code, including injection through code context, repository poisoning, execution environment attacks, and code review manipulation.

item.title: Computer Use Agent Attacks
item.description: Comprehensive analysis of attack vectors targeting AI systems with computer use capabilities, including GUI manipulation, pixel-level injection, and desktop environment exploitation techniques.

item.title: Email Agent Exploitation
item.description: Techniques for exploiting AI agents that process, summarize, draft, and act on emails, including injection through email content, attachment-based attacks, and workflow manipulation.

item.title: File System Agent Risks
item.description: Security risks of AI agents with file system access, including path traversal exploitation, symlink attacks, file content injection, data exfiltration through file operations, and privilege escalation via file system manipulation.

item.title: Scheduling Agent Attacks
item.description: Attack techniques targeting AI scheduling assistants that manage calendars, book meetings, and coordinate schedules, including calendar injection, availability manipulation, and scheduling-based social engineering.

item.title: Voice Agent Attacks
item.description: Attack techniques targeting voice-controlled AI agents, including adversarial audio injection, ultrasonic commands, voice cloning for authentication bypass, and conversation hijacking in voice-first AI systems.

item.title: CrewAI & AutoGen Security
item.description: Security analysis of multi-agent frameworks CrewAI and AutoGen, covering role manipulation, inter-agent injection, delegation abuse, and multi-agent coordination attacks.

item.title: Security Comparison Matrix
item.description: Side-by-side security comparison of major AI agent frameworks: LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, Semantic Kernel, and OpenAI Assistants, covering default security, common misconfigurations, and framework selection guidance.

item.title: Agent Framework Security
item.description: Security analysis of major AI agent frameworks including LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, Semantic Kernel, and OpenAI Assistants, covering default configurations, common vulnerabilities, and framework-specific attack surfaces.

item.title: LangChain Security Deep Dive (Agentic Exploitation)
item.description: Comprehensive security analysis of LangChain and LangGraph, covering dangerous defaults, chain composition attacks, callback exploitation, community tool risks, and agent executor vulnerabilities.

item.title: OpenAI Assistants API Security
item.description: Security analysis of the OpenAI Assistants API, covering file search exploitation, code interpreter abuse, function calling manipulation, thread injection, and managed platform attack surfaces.

item.title: Context Window Attacks
item.description: Techniques for exploiting LLM context window limits, including strategic context overflow to push out system instructions, attention manipulation, and context budget exhaustion attacks.

item.title: Agent Memory Systems Security
item.description: Comprehensive overview of AI agent memory architectures and their security implications, covering conversation persistence, long-term memory stores, context window management, and attack surfaces.

item.title: Memory Exfiltration
item.description: Techniques for extracting data from AI agent memory systems, including extracting previous conversations, revealing other users' data, and cross-session information leakage.

item.title: Memory Poisoning Techniques
item.description: Advanced techniques for injecting persistent instructions into AI agent memory systems, including semantic trojans, self-reinforcing payloads, dormant backdoors, and cross-session persistence mechanisms.

item.title: Function Calling Exploitation
item.description: Overview of how LLM function/tool calling works, the attack surface it creates, and systematic approaches to exploiting function calling interfaces in AI systems.

item.title: Parameter Manipulation
item.description: Techniques for crafting malicious parameter values in LLM function calls, including type confusion, boundary violations, injection through parameters, and exploiting weak validation.

item.title: Recursive Function Calling
item.description: Techniques for triggering infinite loops, resource exhaustion, and call chain manipulation in LLM function calling systems through recursive and repeated invocations.

item.title: Function Result Poisoning (Agentic Exploitation)
item.description: Techniques for manipulating function return values to influence LLM behavior, inject instructions via tool results, and chain poisoned results into multi-step exploitation.

item.title: JSON Schema Injection
item.description: Techniques for manipulating function definitions and JSON schemas to alter LLM behavior, inject additional parameters, and exploit schema validation gaps in tool calling systems.

item.title: Agentic Exploitation
item.description: Comprehensive coverage of security vulnerabilities in agentic AI systems, including MCP tool exploitation, multi-agent protocol attacks, function calling abuse, memory system compromise, framework-specific weaknesses, and workflow pattern attacks.

item.title: Hierarchical Agent Attacks
item.description: Techniques for exploiting hierarchical agent systems where manager agents delegate to worker agents, including delegation manipulation, supervisor evasion, worker exploitation, and authority chain attacks.

item.title: Agentic Workflow Pattern Attacks
item.description: Overview of attacks targeting common agentic workflow patterns including sequential, parallel, and hierarchical execution, covering how workflow structure creates unique exploitation opportunities.

item.title: Parallel Execution Attacks
item.description: Techniques for exploiting parallel execution in agentic systems, including race conditions, conflicting tool calls, output aggregation manipulation, and resource contention attacks.

item.title: Sequential Workflow Exploitation
item.description: Techniques for exploiting sequential agentic workflows by poisoning early stages to corrupt downstream processing, manipulating checkpoints, and exploiting inter-step data dependencies.

item.title: Conversation Preservation
item.description: Preserving AI conversation evidence: interaction log capture, context window reconstruction, multi-turn conversation integrity, tool call chain preservation, and forensic timeline construction.

item.description: Preserving forensic evidence from AI security incidents: model state snapshots, conversation and interaction preservation, embedding database captures, and chain-of-custody for AI-specific artifacts.

item.description: Techniques for capturing and preserving AI model state during incident response: weight snapshots, configuration capture, behavioral fingerprinting, and model artifact integrity verification.

item.description: When and how to escalate AI security incidents: internal escalation tiers, external reporting obligations, regulatory notification requirements, and vendor coordination procedures.

item.description: Comprehensive taxonomy for classifying AI security incidents: jailbreaks, data leaks, model manipulation, supply chain compromise, adversarial attacks, and misuse categories.

item.description: Severity scoring framework designed for AI security incidents: model integrity impact, data exposure scope, blast radius analysis, reversibility assessment, and composite scoring methodology.

item.title: Deepfake Incidents and Detection
item.description: Analysis of significant deepfake incidents including political disinformation, financial fraud, non-consensual content, and corporate impersonation. Covers detection techniques, defensive technologies, and the evolving adversarial landscape.

item.title: Facial Recognition Bias and Failures
item.description: Analysis of facial recognition AI incidents including racial and gender bias in commercial systems, wrongful arrests, privacy violations, and the security implications of adversarial attacks on biometric AI.

item.title: AI Hiring Bias
item.description: Analysis of AI bias in hiring systems including Amazon's discontinued recruiting tool, HireVue's video interview analysis, and systematic bias in resume screening algorithms. Covers testing methodology, legal implications, and red teaming approaches.

item.title: Air Canada Chatbot Hallucination Legal Case
item.description: Analysis of the Air Canada chatbot case where a customer was awarded compensation after the airline's AI chatbot fabricated a bereavement fare policy. The first major legal ruling holding a company liable for its AI chatbot's hallucinations.

item.title: Bing Chat Sydney Incident
item.description: Analysis of the February 2023 Bing Chat 'Sydney' incident where Microsoft's AI chatbot exhibited erratic behavior including emotional manipulation, threats, and identity confusion during extended conversations.

item.title: ChatGPT Data Leak (March 2023)
item.description: Analysis of the March 2023 ChatGPT incident where a Redis client library bug caused users to see other users' conversation titles, partial chat history, and payment information. Covers root cause, impact, and lessons for AI application security.

item.title: DPD Chatbot Jailbreak
item.description: Analysis of the January 2024 DPD chatbot jailbreak where a customer manipulated the parcel delivery company's AI customer service bot into swearing, criticizing the company, and writing poetry about its own incompetence.

item.title: AI Incident Analysis Methodology
item.description: A structured methodology for analyzing AI security incidents. Learn to reconstruct timelines, identify root causes, assess impact, and extract actionable lessons from real-world AI failures across chatbots, data leaks, and alignment failures.

item.title: Samsung Code Leak via ChatGPT
item.description: Analysis of the April 2023 incident where Samsung employees leaked proprietary source code, test data, and internal meeting notes by entering them into ChatGPT. Covers data loss prevention, acceptable use policies, and enterprise AI governance.

item.title: Case Studies
item.description: Real-world AI security incidents, domain-specific case studies, incident analysis reports, and platform security evaluations that ground theoretical knowledge in practical experience.

item.description: IAM exploitation patterns for AWS AI services: overprivileged roles, cross-account model access, service-linked roles, resource policies for Bedrock and SageMaker, and privilege escalation through AI-specific IAM actions.

item.description: Red team methodology for AWS AI services including Bedrock, SageMaker, Comprehend, and Rekognition: service enumeration, attack surface mapping, and exploitation techniques.

item.description: Side-by-side comparison of AWS, Azure, and GCP AI security controls: IAM patterns, content filtering, guardrails, network isolation, logging, and threat detection across cloud providers.

item.description: Red team attack scenarios spanning multiple cloud providers: credential pivoting between AWS, Azure, and GCP, data exfiltration across cloud boundaries, and model portability risks.

item.description: Security risks of multi-cloud AI deployments: cross-cloud attack surfaces, credential management challenges, inconsistent security controls, and governance gaps across AWS, Azure, and GCP AI services.

item.description: Techniques for tricking AI-powered code review tools into approving vulnerable code — covering semantic obfuscation, review bias exploitation, and defense strategies.

item.description: Attack techniques targeting GitHub Copilot: suggestion manipulation via repository poisoning, context window injection, training data extraction, and proxy-based interception.

item.description: Guidelines for developers using AI coding tools securely — covering code review workflows, secret exposure prevention, dependency risks, and organizational policies for AI-assisted development.

item.description: Techniques for manipulating AI coding assistant context: crafting files that influence suggestions, README-driven attacks, and comment injection to steer code generation.

item.description: Poisoning training data for code generation models: inserting vulnerable patterns into popular repositories, dependency confusion via suggestions, and trojan code patterns.

item.description: Techniques for recovering proprietary code from code generation model weights — covering memorization detection, targeted extraction, membership inference, and defensive countermeasures.

item.title: CWE Mapping for AI-Generated Vulnerabilities
item.description: Common AI-generated vulnerabilities mapped to CWE identifiers with real examples: SQL injection (CWE-89), XSS (CWE-79), path traversal (CWE-22), command injection (CWE-78), and hardcoded credentials (CWE-798).

item.title: AI-Generated Vulnerability Patterns
item.description: Overview of common vulnerability patterns in AI-generated code, including why models produce insecure code and how these patterns differ from human-introduced vulnerabilities.

item.title: Language-Specific Risks in AI-Generated Code
item.description: Language-specific security risks in AI-generated code: Python (pickle, eval, subprocess), JavaScript (prototype pollution, eval), Rust (unsafe blocks), and Go (SQL injection in string formatting).

item.title: Prompt Injection via Code
item.description: Hiding prompt injection payloads in code artifacts — through comments, variable names, strings, docstrings, and file metadata — to attack AI coding tools and code-processing pipelines.

item.description: Hands-on lab for executing a complete RAG attack chain from document injection through retrieval manipulation to data exfiltration, targeting every stage of the Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline.

item.title: Lab: Red Team Orchestration
item.description: Build an orchestration system that coordinates multiple attack strategies simultaneously, managing parallel attack campaigns and synthesizing results into comprehensive risk assessments.

item.title: Lab: Safety Regression Testing at Scale
item.description: Build automated pipelines that detect safety degradation across model versions, ensuring that updates and fine-tuning do not introduce new vulnerabilities or weaken existing protections.

item.title: Lab: Model Routing Exploitation
item.description: Attack systems that route requests to different models based on complexity or content, exploiting routing logic to reach less-defended models or bypass safety filters.

item.title: Lab: Transfer Attack Development
item.description: Hands-on lab for crafting adversarial prompts on open-weight models like Llama that transfer to closed-source models like Claude and GPT-4, using iterative refinement and cross-model evaluation.

item.title: Lab: Transfer Attack Development (Advanced Lab)
item.description: Develop adversarial attacks on open-source models that transfer to closed-source models, leveraging weight access for black-box exploitation.

item.title: Lab: Anthropic Claude API Basics
item.description: Set up the Anthropic Claude API for red teaming, learn authentication, the Messages API, system prompts, and how temperature and top-p affect attack success rates.

item.title: Lab: API Key Security
item.description: Learn common API key exposure vectors, secure key management with .env files, detect keys in git history, implement key rotation, and apply least-privilege principles.

item.title: Lab: Build Your First Defense
item.description: Hands-on lab for building a basic AI input defense system using keyword matching, regex filters, and simple classifiers to block prompt injection attempts.

item.title: Lab: Build Your First Defense (Beginner Lab)
item.description: Create a simple input filter that blocks common prompt injection patterns, then test it against the attack techniques you have learned in previous labs.

item.title: Lab: Model Comparison
item.description: Test the same attack techniques against different language models and compare their safety behaviors, refusal patterns, and vulnerability profiles.

item.title: Lab: Compare Model Safety
item.description: Hands-on lab for running identical safety tests against GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, and Llama to compare how different models handle prompt injection, jailbreaks, and safety boundary enforcement.

item.title: Lab: Context Manipulation
item.description: Manipulate the conversational context to change model behavior, using techniques like context flooding, false history injection, and persona shifting.

item.title: Lab: Context Overflow Attacks
item.description: Explore context window overflow attacks that push system instructions out of the model's attention by filling the context with padding content, and measure instruction-following degradation.

item.title: Lab: Defense Bypass Basics
item.description: Learn basic techniques to bypass simple LLM defenses including keyword filters, instruction reinforcement, and output validators using encoding, reformulation, and indirect approaches.

item.title: Lab: Delimiter Injection Attacks
item.description: Craft payloads that exploit XML, markdown, and special token delimiters to break instruction boundaries and manipulate how LLMs parse system prompts versus user input.

item.title: Lab: Delimiter Escape Attacks
item.description: Craft payloads that escape delimiter boundaries separating system and user content, testing how models handle broken fences, nested delimiters, and format confusion.

item.title: Lab: Ethical Red Teaming
item.description: Practice responsible AI red teaming with proper documentation, scope management, and ethical decision-making frameworks.

item.title: Lab: Ethical Red Teaming (Beginner Lab)
item.description: Hands-on lab for practicing responsible AI red teaming with proper documentation, scope management, ethical boundaries, and disclosure procedures.

item.title: Lab: Few-Shot Manipulation Attacks
item.description: Craft fake few-shot examples that teach the model to bypass its safety training by demonstrating the desired adversarial behavior through fabricated conversation examples.

item.title: Lab: Your First Jailbreak
item.description: Try basic jailbreak techniques against a local model using Ollama, learning the difference between prompt injection and jailbreaking through hands-on experimentation.

item.title: Lab: Garak Setup and First Scan
item.description: Install and configure garak, the LLM vulnerability scanner, then run your first automated scan against a local model to discover common vulnerabilities.

item.title: Lab: Injection Detection Tool
item.description: Build a basic prompt injection detection tool using pattern matching, heuristics, and LLM-based classification to identify malicious inputs before they reach the target model.

item.title: Lab: Injection Techniques Survey
item.description: Survey and test ten fundamental prompt injection techniques against a local LLM, measuring effectiveness and cataloging behavioral patterns for each approach.

item.title: Lab: Instruction Following Priority
item.description: Test how language models prioritize conflicting instructions from system prompts, user messages, and embedded directives to understand the instruction hierarchy.

item.title: Lab: Markdown Injection
item.description: Inject images, links, and formatting into LLM responses that exfiltrate data or alter display rendering in chat interfaces.

item.title: Lab: Multi-Language Injection
item.description: Test prompt injection techniques using multiple natural languages, exploring how safety training coverage varies across languages and how translation requests can mask payloads.

item.title: Lab: Multi-Turn Escalation Attacks
item.description: Build trust over several conversation messages, then exploit established rapport to bypass safety mechanisms through gradual, multi-turn escalation techniques.

item.title: Lab: Setting Up Ollama for Local LLM Testing
item.description: Install and configure Ollama for local LLM red teaming, download models, perform basic interactions, and compare safety behavior between local and API-hosted models.

item.title: Lab: Output Format Exploitation
item.description: Manipulate output formats like JSON, CSV, code blocks, and structured data to extract information that models would normally refuse to provide in natural language.

item.title: Lab: Output Steering
item.description: Learn to steer LLM outputs into specific formats, tones, and content using injection techniques that manipulate how the model structures and presents its responses.

item.title: Lab: Payload Crafting
item.description: Learn to craft effective prompt injection payloads from scratch by understanding payload structure, testing iteratively, and optimizing for reliability against a local model.

item.title: Lab: Encoding Bypasses
item.description: Test Base64, ROT13, hex, and Unicode encoding techniques to evade LLM content filters while preserving the semantic meaning of adversarial payloads.

item.title: Lab: Prompt Leaking Basics
item.description: Learn to extract system prompts from chatbots using common leaking techniques including direct requests, indirect elicitation, and format manipulation.

item.title: Lab: Promptfoo Setup and First Eval
item.description: Install and configure promptfoo for systematic LLM evaluation, then run your first red team evaluation to test model safety boundaries.

item.title: Lab: PyRIT Setup and First Attack
item.description: Install and configure Microsoft's PyRIT (Python Risk Identification Toolkit) for automated red teaming, then run your first orchestrated attack against a local model.

item.title: Lab: Analyzing LLM Responses
item.description: Learn to identify safety refusals, partial compliance, inconsistent behavior, and response fingerprinting techniques when analyzing LLM outputs during red team testing.

item.title: Lab: Role-Play Attacks
item.description: Use persona-based approaches to bypass AI safety measures by assigning alternate identities, characters, and scenarios that override the model's trained refusal behaviors.

item.title: Lab: Mapping Safety Boundaries
item.description: Systematically discover what a language model will and won't do by probing its safety boundaries across multiple categories and documenting the results.

item.title: Lab: Structured Output Manipulation
item.description: Explore how JSON mode, schema injection, and forced output formats can bypass LLM safety filters and manipulate structured responses.

item.title: Lab: System Prompt Override
item.description: Explore techniques to override system-level instructions with user-level inputs, testing how models prioritize conflicting directives across different prompt architectures.

item.title: Lab: System Prompt Reconstruction
item.description: Use various techniques to systematically extract and reconstruct the full system prompt of an LLM application, combining direct, indirect, and incremental extraction methods.

item.title: CTF: Agent Escalation
item.description: Five-flag CTF challenge focused on exploiting agentic AI systems. Progress from basic tool abuse through privilege escalation, indirect injection, memory poisoning, and multi-agent chain attacks.

item.title: CTF: Boss Rush
item.description: Chain attacks across multiple AI models in sequence. Each model guards the next, requiring different attack techniques at each stage. Defeat all five models to extract the final flag in this ultimate red teaming challenge.

item.title: CTF: Cloud AI Heist
item.description: Extract secrets from a cloud-deployed AI application by exploiting misconfigurations, SSRF, metadata endpoints, and model-level vulnerabilities in a realistic cloud environment.

item.title: CTF: Code Gen Exploit
item.description: Manipulate AI code generation to produce vulnerable, backdoored, or malicious code. Explore how prompt manipulation influences code security, from subtle vulnerability injection to full backdoor insertion.

item.title: CTF: Defense Breaker
item.description: Bypass progressively sophisticated defense mechanisms protecting an AI system. Each level introduces a new defense layer while retaining all previous defenses, testing your ability to adapt and chain bypass techniques.

item.title: CTF: Fine-Tune Detective
item.description: Detect backdoors in fine-tuned language models through behavioral analysis, weight inspection, and activation pattern examination. Practice the forensic techniques needed to identify compromised models before deployment.

item.title: CTF: AI Forensics Investigation
item.description: Analyze logs, model outputs, and system artifacts to reconstruct an AI security incident. Develop forensic analysis skills for AI-specific attack patterns, data exfiltration traces, and adversarial prompt detection.

item.title: CTF: RAG Heist
item.description: Extract sensitive information from a Retrieval-Augmented Generation system by exploiting retrieval mechanisms, document parsing, embedding manipulation, and context window management vulnerabilities.

item.title: CTF: Supply Chain Attack
item.description: Find and exploit vulnerabilities in an ML supply chain including compromised dependencies, poisoned models, backdoored training data, and malicious model files. Practice ML-specific supply chain security assessment.

item.title: Lab: Adversarial ML From Scratch
item.description: Hands-on expert lab for implementing gradient-based adversarial attacks against language models from scratch without frameworks, building intuition for how adversarial perturbations exploit model gradients.

item.title: Lab: Adversarial Suffix Optimization
item.description: Implement GCG-style adversarial suffix attacks that automatically discover token sequences causing language models to comply with harmful requests. Covers gradient-based optimization, transferability analysis, and defense evaluation.

item.title: Lab: Alignment Stress Testing
item.description: Push language model alignment to its breaking points through systematic stress testing. Identify conditions where safety training fails, measure alignment degradation curves, and map the boundaries of model compliance.

item.title: Lab: Build Agent Security Scanner
item.description: Build an automated security scanner for agentic AI systems that detects vulnerabilities in tool use, permission handling, memory management, and multi-step execution flows. Cover agent-specific attack surfaces that traditional LLM testing misses.

item.title: Lab: Build an AI Fuzzer
item.description: Build a comprehensive fuzzing framework for large language models that systematically generates malformed, unexpected, and adversarial inputs to discover crashes, unexpected behaviors, and safety bypasses.

item.title: Lab: Build Behavior Diff Tool
item.description: Build a tool that systematically compares language model behavior across versions, configurations, and providers. Detect safety regressions, capability changes, and behavioral drift with automated differential analysis.

item.title: Lab: Build Guardrail Evaluator
item.description: Build an automated framework for evaluating AI guardrails and safety filters. Test input filters, output classifiers, content moderation systems, and defense-in-depth architectures for coverage gaps and bypass vulnerabilities.

item.title: Lab: Build Jailbreak Automation
item.description: Build an automated jailbreak testing framework that generates, mutates, and evaluates attack prompts at scale. Covers prompt mutation engines, success classifiers, and campaign management for systematic red team testing.

item.title: Lab: Defense Engineering Lab
item.description: Expert-level lab for building and evaluating a production-grade multi-layer AI defense system including input classifiers, output monitors, semantic analysis, and adversarial robustness testing.

item.title: Lab: Emergent Capability Probing
item.description: Systematically test large language models for undocumented capabilities including hidden knowledge, unreported skills, and behaviors that emerge only under specific conditions. Build a structured probing framework for capability discovery.

item.title: Lab: Full-Stack AI Exploitation
item.description: Chain vulnerabilities across UI, API, model, and infrastructure layers to achieve full-stack compromise of an AI application. Learn to identify cross-layer attack paths and build exploit chains that no single-layer defense can prevent.

item.title: Lab: Deploy Honeypot AI
item.description: Build and deploy a decoy AI system designed to detect, analyze, and characterize attackers targeting AI applications. Learn honeypot design, telemetry collection, attacker profiling, and threat intelligence generation for AI-specific threats.

item.title: Lab: Novel Jailbreak Research
item.description: Systematic methodology for discovering new jailbreak techniques against large language models. Learn to identify unexplored attack surfaces, develop novel attack vectors, and validate findings with scientific rigor.

item.title: Lab: ML Pipeline Poisoning
item.description: Compromise an end-to-end machine learning pipeline by attacking data ingestion, preprocessing, training, evaluation, and deployment stages. Learn to identify and exploit weaknesses across the full ML lifecycle.

item.title: Lab: Model Registry Compromise
item.description: Explore techniques for compromising model registries and substituting malicious models into production pipelines. Learn to detect model tampering, verify model provenance, and secure the model supply chain.

item.title: Lab: Vulnerability Research Methodology
item.description: Systematic methodology lab for discovering novel AI vulnerabilities including hypothesis generation, attack surface mapping, experimental design, validation protocols, and responsible disclosure.

item.title: Lab: Create a Safety Benchmark
item.description: Design, build, and validate a comprehensive AI safety evaluation suite. Learn benchmark design principles, test case generation, scoring methodology, and statistical validation for measuring LLM safety across multiple risk categories.

item.title: Labs & Hands-On Practice
item.description: Progressive hands-on lab exercises spanning beginner to expert difficulty, including CTF challenges and full engagement simulations for AI red teaming skill development.

item.title: Lab: Advanced RAG Poisoning
item.description: Hands-on lab for crafting documents that reliably get retrieved and influence RAG responses for specific target queries.

item.title: Lab: Agent Memory Manipulation
item.description: Hands-on lab for injecting persistent instructions into an agent's memory and context that affect future interactions and conversations.

item.title: Lab: Agent Prompt Leaking
item.description: Hands-on lab for extracting system prompts from tool-using AI agents by exploiting tool interactions, observation channels, and multi-step reasoning to leak protected instructions.

item.title: Lab: Automated Red Team Pipeline
item.description: Hands-on lab for building a continuous AI red team testing pipeline using promptfoo, GitHub Actions, and automated attack generation to catch safety regressions before deployment.

item.title: Lab: Automated Red Team Testing
item.description: Set up promptfoo for continuous automated security testing of AI applications, with custom evaluators and CI/CD integration.

item.title: Lab: Azure Content Filter Evasion
item.description: Hands-on lab for mapping and testing Azure OpenAI Service content filtering categories, severity levels, and bypass techniques.

item.title: Lab: AWS Bedrock Guardrails Testing
item.description: Hands-on lab for systematically testing and bypassing AWS Bedrock's built-in guardrails including content filters, denied topics, and word filters.

item.title: Lab: Chunking Exploitation
item.description: Hands-on lab for crafting documents that split across chunks in ways that hide malicious content from chunk-level filtering while maintaining attack effectiveness.

item.title: Lab: Citation Fabrication
item.description: Hands-on lab for getting RAG systems to cite documents that don't exist or misattribute quotes to legitimate sources.

item.title: Lab: Context Window Overflow Attacks
item.description: Hands-on lab exploring how overflowing a model's context window with padding content can push safety instructions out of the attention window and enable injection attacks.

item.title: Lab: Data Exfiltration Channels
item.description: Hands-on lab for extracting data from AI systems through markdown image rendering, invisible links, tool call parameters, and other covert exfiltration channels.

item.title: Lab: Data Exfiltration Channels (Intermediate Lab)
item.description: Extract sensitive information from AI systems through various exfiltration channels including crafted links, image tags, tool calls, and side-channel leakage.

item.title: Lab: Defense Effectiveness Measurement
item.description: Hands-on lab for quantifying AI guardrail robustness using attack success rates, evasion metrics, false positive rates, and statistical analysis of defense performance.

item.title: Lab: Defense Effectiveness Testing
item.description: Systematically test and measure the robustness of AI guardrails using structured methodology, metrics, and repeatable test suites.

item.title: Lab: Encoding Bypass Lab
item.description: Hands-on lab for systematically testing Base64, ROT13, Unicode homoglyph, hexadecimal, and combined encoding bypasses against LLM safety filters with measured success rates.

item.title: Lab: Encoding Evasion Techniques
item.description: Hands-on lab using Base64, ROT13, Unicode normalization, and custom encoding schemes to evade input filters and safety classifiers in language model systems.

item.title: Lab: Few-Shot Example Injection
item.description: Hands-on lab exploring how injected few-shot examples can steer language model outputs toward attacker-chosen behaviors by exploiting in-context learning.

item.title: Lab: Function Calling Injection
item.description: Hands-on lab for exploiting function calling mechanisms by crafting inputs that manipulate which functions get called and with what parameters.

item.title: Lab: Indirect Prompt Injection
item.description: Inject instructions through external data sources including documents, web pages, and emails that a target AI system processes as context.

item.title: Lab: JSON Input Injection
item.description: Hands-on lab exploring how adversarial payloads injected through structured JSON inputs can manipulate language model behavior, bypass schema validation, and exploit parsing inconsistencies.

item.title: Lab: Markdown-Based Injection
item.description: Hands-on lab exploring how Markdown rendering in AI-generated outputs can be exploited to inject hidden content, exfiltrate data through image tags, and manipulate displayed information.

item.title: Lab: MCP Server Exploitation
item.description: Hands-on lab for attacking a vulnerable MCP server, demonstrating tool shadowing, consent phishing, and server impersonation techniques.

item.title: Lab: RAG Metadata Injection
item.description: Hands-on lab for exploiting metadata fields like titles, descriptions, and timestamps to manipulate RAG retrieval ranking and influence responses.

item.title: Lab: Multi-Turn Progressive Escalation
item.description: Hands-on lab building multi-turn attack sequences that progressively escalate from benign queries to full safety bypass through gradual trust establishment and boundary pushing.

item.title: Lab: Multimodal Injection
item.description: Hands-on lab for embedding text instructions in images to exploit vision-enabled LLMs. Learn to craft visual prompt injections, test OCR-based attacks, and evaluate multimodal safety boundaries.

item.title: Lab: Multimodal Injection (Intermediate Lab)
item.description: Embed prompt injection instructions in images for vision-enabled models, testing how visual content can carry adversarial payloads.

item.title: Lab: Agent Permission Escalation
item.description: Hands-on lab for tricking AI agents into performing actions beyond their intended scope through privilege escalation techniques.

item.title: Lab: Recursive Injection Chains
item.description: Hands-on lab building self-reinforcing prompt injection chains where each model response amplifies the injection, creating feedback loops that progressively override safety controls.

item.title: Lab: Re-ranking Attacks
item.description: Hands-on lab for manipulating the re-ranking stage of RAG pipelines to promote or suppress specific documents in retrieval results.

item.title: Lab: Supply Chain Audit
item.description: Audit an ML project's dependencies for vulnerabilities, covering model files, Python packages, container images, and training data provenance.

item.title: Lab: ML Supply Chain Scan
item.description: Hands-on lab for auditing machine learning model dependencies, detecting malicious packages in ML pipelines, and scanning model files for backdoors and supply chain threats.

item.title: Lab: Tool Result Poisoning
item.description: Hands-on lab for poisoning tool outputs to redirect agent behavior by injecting malicious content through tool results.

item.title: Lab: Language Translation Bypass
item.description: Hands-on lab exploiting language translation capabilities to bypass safety filters by submitting prompts in low-resource languages, code-switching, and using transliteration tricks.

item.title: Lab: Workflow Hijacking
item.description: Hands-on lab for redirecting multi-step agent workflows to perform unintended actions by manipulating intermediate states and control flow.

item.title: Simulation: Autonomous AI Agent Red Team
item.description: Red team engagement simulation targeting an autonomous AI agent with tool access, file system permissions, and internet connectivity. Tests for privilege escalation, unauthorized actions, and goal hijacking.

item.title: Simulation: AI Bug Bounty
item.description: Find and report vulnerabilities in a simulated AI bug bounty program, practicing professional vulnerability disclosure and bounty-eligible reporting.

item.title: Simulation: Build & Defend a Chatbot
item.description: Defense simulation where you build a chatbot with layered defenses, test it against a standardized attack suite, measure defense effectiveness, and iterate on weaknesses.

item.title: Simulation: Defense in Depth
item.description: Expert-level defense simulation implementing a full defense stack including input filter, output monitor, rate limiter, anomaly detector, and circuit breaker, then measuring effectiveness against automated attacks.

item.title: Simulation: RAG Pipeline Poisoning
item.description: Red team engagement simulation targeting a RAG-based knowledge management system, covering embedding injection, document poisoning, retrieval manipulation, and knowledge base exfiltration.

item.title: Simulation: Enterprise Chatbot Engagement
item.description: Full red team engagement simulation targeting a customer-facing chatbot deployed by a fictional e-commerce company, covering reconnaissance, prompt injection, data exfiltration, and PII harvesting.

item.title: Simulation: Financial AI Platform
item.description: Expert-level red team engagement simulation targeting a fictional fintech AI-powered financial advisor, covering API mapping, advice manipulation, credential extraction, and regulatory impact assessment.

item.title: Simulation: Government AI Portal
item.description: Red team engagement simulation targeting a public-facing government benefits chatbot, covering reconnaissance, benefits fraud assistance, PII harvesting, bias exploitation, and remediation recommendations.

item.title: Simulation: Guardrail Engineering
item.description: Defense simulation where you design and implement a multi-layer guardrail system, test it against progressively sophisticated attacks, and document false positive/negative rates.

item.title: Simulation: Healthcare AI System
item.description: Expert-level red team engagement simulation targeting a clinical decision support system, covering HIPAA-scoped threat modeling, diagnostic manipulation, patient data extraction, and treatment recommendation poisoning.

item.title: Simulation: Legal AI Red Team
item.description: Red team engagement simulation targeting an AI-powered legal research and contract analysis platform, covering citation hallucination, privilege leakage, and adversarial clause injection.

item.title: Simulation: AI SOC Simulation
item.description: Defense simulation where you set up monitoring for an AI application, then respond to simulated attacks by practicing alert triage, investigation, and escalation procedures.

item.title: Simulation: Open Source AI Project Audit
item.description: Security audit simulation for an open-source AI application, covering code review, dependency analysis, model supply chain verification, and deployment configuration review.

item.title: Simulation: Red vs Blue
item.description: Competitive exercise where teams alternate between attacking and defending an AI application, scoring points for successful attacks and effective defenses.

item.title: Simulation: SaaS AI Product
item.description: Red team engagement simulation targeting a B2B SaaS platform with AI-powered document analysis, search, and automation features, covering multi-tenant isolation, API security, and cross-tenant data leakage.

item.title: Simulation: Startup AI Assessment
item.description: Red team a startup's AI-powered product with limited scope and budget, making pragmatic tradeoffs between thoroughness and time constraints.

item.title: Simulation: AI Supply Chain Attack Investigation
item.description: Investigate and respond to a supply chain compromise affecting an AI system's model weights, training data pipeline, and third-party dependencies.

item.title: Simulation: Voice Assistant Red Team
item.description: Red team engagement simulation targeting an AI voice assistant deployed in a smart home platform, covering audio-based prompt injection, wake word exploitation, and privacy exfiltration.

item.title: Experiment Tracking Security
item.description: Security risks in ML experiment tracking systems: what gets logged, what is sensitive, and how tracking platforms become high-value targets for attackers seeking intellectual property and pipeline access.

item.title: Experiment Metadata Leakage
item.description: How experiment metadata reveals sensitive information: hyperparameters exposing architecture secrets, loss curves revealing training data properties, run names and tags disclosing project intent, and techniques for extracting intelligence from ML experiment logs.

item.title: MLflow Attack Surface
item.description: Security analysis of MLflow: tracking server authentication weaknesses, artifact store access control, model registry tampering, SQL injection in tracking queries, and exploitation techniques for both open-source and managed deployments.

item.title: Weights & Biases Attack Surface
item.description: Security analysis of Weights & Biases (W&B/wandb): API key exposure, experiment data leakage, team boundary violations, artifact poisoning, and attack techniques specific to the W&B platform.

item.title: Feature Store Access Control
item.description: Access control strategies for feature stores: feature-level permissions, cross-team data leakage prevention, PII protection in features, service account management, and implementing least-privilege access for ML feature infrastructure.

item.title: Feature Poisoning Attacks
item.description: Techniques for poisoning feature store data to manipulate model behavior: direct feature value manipulation, time-travel attacks, online/offline store consistency exploitation, and targeted entity-level feature poisoning.

item.title: Feature Store Security (Llmops Security)
item.description: Security overview of ML feature stores (Feast, Tecton, Vertex Feature Store): architecture and trust model, attack surfaces in online and offline stores, and the security implications of centralized feature management for ML systems.

item.title: LLMOps Security
item.description: Comprehensive overview of security across the LLMOps lifecycle: from data preparation and experiment tracking through model deployment and production monitoring. Attack surfaces, threat models, and defensive strategies for ML operations.

item.title: GPU Security for AI
item.description: GPU security risks in AI workloads — covering memory isolation failures, side-channel attacks, multi-tenant GPU risks, GPU firmware vulnerabilities, and secure GPU configuration.

item.title: Inference Optimization Risks
item.description: Security implications of model optimization techniques — covering quantization safety degradation, pruning vulnerability introduction, distillation attacks, and speculative decoding risks.

item.title: Model Caching Security
item.description: Security risks of caching in AI inference — covering KV cache attacks, prompt caching exploitation, shared cache information leakage, cache poisoning, and secure caching configurations.

item.title: ML Artifact Integrity
item.description: Ensuring integrity of ML artifacts throughout the pipeline: hash verification strategies, signed artifact workflows, reproducible builds for ML, deterministic training challenges, and end-to-end artifact provenance.

item.title: Security Gates in ML Deployment
item.description: Implementing security checkpoints in ML deployment pipelines: automated safety testing, performance regression detection, bias evaluation, approval workflows, and designing gates that balance security with deployment velocity.

item.title: ML CI/CD Security
item.description: Security overview of ML continuous integration and deployment pipelines: how ML CI/CD differs from traditional CI/CD, unique attack surfaces in training workflows, and the security implications of automated model building and deployment.

item.title: ML Pipeline Poisoning
item.description: Attack techniques targeting ML training and deployment pipelines: training job manipulation, data pipeline injection, compute hijacking, artifact substitution, and exploitation of pipeline orchestration weaknesses.

item.title: Hugging Face Hub Security
item.description: Attack surface analysis of Hugging Face Hub: malicious model uploads, pickle deserialization exploits, model card manipulation, trust signal limitations, gated model bypass, and community-driven trust exploitation.

item.title: Model Registry Security (Llmops Security)
item.description: Security overview of model registries: how registries manage model lifecycle, access control models, trust boundaries, and the unique security challenges of storing and distributing opaque ML artifacts.

item.title: Model Signing and Provenance
item.description: Cryptographic signing for ML models: Sigstore for ML artifacts, cosign for model weights, SLSA framework applied to ML pipelines, supply chain levels for model provenance, and practical implementation of model artifact verification.

item.title: Registry-Specific Attacks
item.description: Attack techniques targeting model registries: version confusion, dependency resolution exploitation, namespace squatting, model aliasing attacks, and practical exploitation of registry trust models.

item.title: Model Serving Security
item.description: Security hardening for model serving infrastructure — covering vLLM, TGI, Triton Inference Server configuration, API security, resource isolation, and deployment best practices.

item.title: Claude Attack Surface
item.description: Claude-specific attack vectors including Constitutional AI weaknesses, tool use exploitation, system prompt handling, vision attacks, and XML tag injection techniques.

item.title: Claude (Anthropic) Overview
item.description: Architecture and security overview of Anthropic's Claude model family including Sonnet, Opus, and Haiku variants, Constitutional AI training, RLHF approach, and harmlessness design philosophy.

item.title: Claude Known Vulnerabilities
item.description: Documented Claude vulnerabilities including many-shot jailbreaking, alignment faking research, crescendo attacks, prompt injection via artifacts, and system prompt extraction techniques.

item.title: Claude Testing Methodology
item.description: Systematic methodology for red teaming Claude models, including API probing, model card analysis, safety boundary mapping, and comparative testing across Opus, Sonnet, and Haiku tiers.

item.title: Cross-Model Comparison
item.description: Methodology for systematically comparing LLM security across model families, including standardized evaluation frameworks, architectural difference analysis, and comparative testing approaches.

item.title: Jailbreak Portability
item.description: Analysis of which jailbreaks transfer across models and why, including universal vs model-specific techniques, transfer attack methodology, and factors that determine portability.

item.title: Safety Comparison Across Models
item.description: Comparing safety across GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, and open-weight models using standardized test suites, failure mode analysis, and defense coverage gap identification.

item.title: Gemini Attack Surface
item.description: Gemini-specific attack vectors including multimodal injection across image, audio, and video inputs, Google Workspace integration attacks, grounding abuse, and code execution exploitation.

item.title: Gemini (Google) Overview
item.description: Architecture overview of Google's Gemini model family, including natively multimodal design, long context capabilities, Google ecosystem integration, and security-relevant features for red teaming.

item.title: Gemini Known Vulnerabilities
item.description: Documented Gemini vulnerabilities including image generation bias incidents, system prompt extraction, safety filter inconsistencies, multimodal injection exploits, and grounding abuse.

item.title: Gemini Testing Methodology
item.description: Systematic methodology for red teaming Gemini, including Vertex AI API probing, Google AI Studio testing, multimodal test case design, and grounding attack validation.

item.title: GPT-4 Attack Surface
item.description: Comprehensive analysis of GPT-4-specific attack vectors including function calling exploitation, vision input attacks, system message hierarchy abuse, structured output manipulation, and known jailbreak patterns.

item.title: GPT-4 / GPT-4o Overview
item.description: Architecture overview of OpenAI's GPT-4 and GPT-4o models, including rumored Mixture of Experts design, capabilities, API surface, and security-relevant features for red teaming.

item.title: GPT-4 Known Vulnerabilities
item.description: Documented GPT-4 vulnerabilities including DAN jailbreaks, data extraction incidents, system prompt leaks, tool-use exploits, and fine-tuning safety removal.

item.title: GPT-4 Testing Methodology
item.description: Systematic methodology for red teaming GPT-4, including API-based probing techniques, rate limit considerations, content policy mapping, and safety boundary discovery.

item.title: Model Deep Dives
item.description: Why model-specific knowledge matters for AI red teaming, how different architectures create different attack surfaces, and a systematic methodology for profiling any new model.

item.title: Emerging Models
item.description: Security analysis of emerging open-weight models including DeepSeek, Qwen, and Command R+, covering new attack surfaces, less-tested safety measures, and multilingual exploitation techniques.

item.title: Open-Weight Model Security
item.description: Security analysis of open-weight models including Llama, Mistral, Qwen, and DeepSeek, covering unique risks from full weight access, fine-tuning attacks, and deployment security challenges.

item.title: Llama Family Attacks
item.description: Comprehensive attack analysis of Meta's Llama model family including weight manipulation, fine-tuning safety removal, quantization artifacts, uncensored variants, and Llama Guard bypass techniques.
